name: Risted Laks Med Kremsaus (Fried Trout in Sour
recipeInstructions:
1. If using frozen trout, defrost them completely before cooking.
2. Wash fish under cold running water, pat dry inside and out, and sprinkle a little salt into the cavities.
3. Spread 1/2 cup flour over wax paper, roll the fish in the flour, and shake off any excess.
4. In a heavy 10-12 inch skillet, heat 2 tablespoons butter and 2 tablespoons oil.
5. When foam subsides, lower heat to moderate and fry the trout, 2 at a time, for about 5 minutes on each side, turning carefully with a large spatula.
6. Keep the fish warm on a heatproof platter in a 200°F oven while making the sauce.
7. Pour off all the fat from the skillet and replace it with 2 tablespoons fresh butter.
8. Stir over low heat, scraping up brown pan drippings with a wooden spoon.
9. Add sour cream and continue stirring for about 3 minutes without letting the cream boil.
10. Stir in lemon juice and pour the sauce over the hot fish.
11. Garnish with chopped parsley and serve at once.
